title: Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS)
concept: Implementing NMS to filter out duplicate, low-confidence boxes overlapping the same object.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of loss functions in object detection?

options.text: They help improve the accuracy of object classification.
They are used only to detect objects, not to classify them.
They serve no purpose in object detection.

exercises.explain: Loss functions guide the model in improving its ability to accurately classify and localize objects.

cards.title: What is Non-Maximum Suppression?
cards.body: Non-Maximum Suppression (NMS) is a technique used to filter out duplicate bounding boxes around the same object.

cards.title: Definition of NMS
cards.body: NMS removes overlapping boxes based on their confidence scores, ensuring only the best box is kept for each detected object.

cards.title: NMS as a Bouncer
cards.body: Think of NMS like a bouncer at a club. If too many people (boxes) are crowded around the same entrance (object), the bouncer ensures that only the most confident guest (highest score) gets in.

cards.title: Example of NMS
cards.body: If three boxes overlap around a cat with confidence scores of 0.9, 0.75, and 0.4, NMS will keep the box with the score of 0.9 and discard the others.

cards.title: Myth: NMS is Not Needed
cards.body: Some might think NMS is unnecessary. However, it is essential for cleaner and more accurate detections.

cards.title: NMS Visual Comparison
cards.body: See how NMS filters boxes effectively!

exercises.prompt: What does NMS do in object detection?

options.text: Keeps all overlapping boxes regardless of confidence scores.
Removes low-confidence overlapping boxes and keeps the best one.
Only works for boxes with the highest size.
Increases the number of bounding boxes for an object detection.

exercises.explain: NMS effectively keeps only the most reliable bounding boxes, ensuring higher accuracy in object detection.

exercises.prompt: True or False: NMS can help to reduce clutter in detected bounding boxes.
exercises.explain: True, NMS helps clean up overlapping detections by removing less confident boxes, leading to clearer results.

exercises.prompt: NMS filters out duplicate boxes by comparing their ____ scores.
exercises.sentence: NMS filters out duplicate boxes by comparing their confidence scores.
exercises.explain: The correct term is 'confidence' as it refers to how sure the model is about each detection.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following best describes the role of Non-Maximum Suppression?

options.text: It always keeps all detections regardless of overlap.
It removes overlapping boxes based on confidence scores.
It adds more boxes around each object detected.

exercises.explain: NMS's primary function is to remove overlapping boxes while preserving the one with the highest confidence score.

exercises.prompt: What is the main benefit of using Non-Maximum Suppression in object detection?

options.text: To ensure all boxes are visible on the output.
To maintain clarity by keeping only the most confident detections.
To increase the number of detected objects in every image.
To find all possible overlaps without consideration of scores.

exercises.explain: The main benefit of NMS is that it maintains the clarity of the detections by ensuring that only the most confident box for each object is shown, reducing confusion caused by duplicate detections.
